talk_metadata.talk_title: Defining a molecular signature for KEAP1-Nrf2-mediated resistance to KRAS inhibition
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we didn't do the experiment, but let me see if this can answer your question"
Speaker said: "we see that SLC7A11 is significantly upregulated, so we try to knock it down... that won't reverse the resistance to RAS inhibitors"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: KRAS inhibitors (G12D inhibitor MRTX1133, multi-RAS inhibitor RMC7977)
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: KEAP1 loss leading to Nrf2 activation drives resistance independent of RAS, MYC, TEAD pathways
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: we found that the KEAP1 knockout cell line become far less sensitive to RAS inhibitor compared to the control cell lines

key_data_gaps: No toxicity assessment of DRP104 combination

crispro_opportunity.description: KEAP1-Nrf2 driven resistance could be targeted by CrisPRO predictive modeling of Nrf2 transcriptional programs to identify patients likely to benefit from glutamine metabolism inhibition.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker concluded that KEAP1 knockout promotes resistance and that a glutamine antagonist DRP104 can overcome it.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to score KEAP1/Nrf2 signature in patient tumors and prioritize combination with glutamine antagonists.

unresolved_questions: What is the durability of the DRP104 + KRAS inhibitor combination in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Selective ULK1/2 inhibitors enhance KRAS pathway blockade and uncover stress adaptive programs
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Hydroxychloroquine and chloroquine have a lot of off‑target effect and are linked to high toxicity"
Speaker said: "we are still missing some specific biomarker in order to pinpoint when and how we can target autophagy"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Off‑target effects of chloroquine obscure on‑target autophagy inhibition
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker highlighted high toxicity of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker‑driven selection for autophagy dependence

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed structure‑guided design and SAR but noted need for biomarker to guide use
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad use without patient selection may dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ULK1 inhibition synergizes with KRAS pathway blockade, yet concluded that without a biomarker the approach may fail in pancreatic cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify transcriptional or metabolic signatures of autophagy dependence to select patients for ULK1 inhibitor + KRAS inhibitor combos.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker admitted missing specific biomarker to pinpoint when and how to target autophagy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics to derive an autophagy‑dependency score for trial enrichment.

cited_competitors.name: Hydroxychloroquine
cited_competitors.context: Compared as off‑target autophagy inhibitor with high toxicity

unresolved_questions: Will ULK1 PROTACs show superior synergy?
What biomarker predicts ULK1 inhibitor response?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Modeling of acquired resistance to multi RAS inhibitor RMC-6236 in KRAS mutant pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see that the Y64 mutation completely abolishes binding"
Speaker said: "it is unlikely to be a single event‑driven resistance"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Secondary KRAS Y64 mutations prevent tri‑complex formation with RAF

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No pre‑screening for KRAS Y64 or other secondary mutations

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker showed that Y64 mutation abolishes binding, indicating a narrow chemical interaction surface
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on single binding mode makes it vulnerable to point mutations

key_data_gaps: No clinical data on Y64 prevalence

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to predict KRAS secondary mutation hotspots (e.g., Y64) and design next‑generation molecular glues that retain binding.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated loss of binding with Y64 mutation.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational modeling of KRAS surface flexibility to guide redesign of glue scaffold.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine pan‑KRAS glue with MEK inhibitors to overcome Y64‑mediated resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted MEK inhibitor remained effective against Y64 mutant lines.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize MEK inhibitor combos based on pathway re‑activation signatures.

cited_competitors.name: Merck KRAS inhibitor
cited_competitors.context: Shown to retain activity against Y64 mutants

unresolved_questions: What is the clinical frequency of KRAS Y64 mutations after RMC‑6236 treatment?
Can combination with SHP2 or RTK inhibitors prevent emergence of bypass pathways?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Combining RAS inhibitors with RNR inhibitor to prevent ecDNA‑driven resistance
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the addition of BBI 825 completely prevented the emergence of resistance"
Speaker said: "we were not able to get any resistant subclone from this combinatorial treatment"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: KRAS G12C inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib) + EGFR antibodies
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: ecDNA‑mediated KRAS or MET amplifications drive rapid resistance
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some patients acquired KRAS amplifications and progressed quickly

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No baseline ecDNA assessment to stratify patients

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Oral selective RNR inhibition may be limited by nucleotide salvage pathways
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Combination relies on suppressing amplification which may not apply to all resistance mechanisms

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that KRAS G12C + cetuximab alone leads to rapid KRAS amplification, yet concluded that adding BBI 825 can fully prevent resistance, despite not testing all possible bypass routes

key_data_gaps: Long‑term clinical outcome data with BBI 825
Effect in MSI‑high tumors where amplification is not the driver

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop CrisPRO‑based ecDNA detection score to enroll patients who would benefit from RNR inhibition combo.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker linked ecDNA‑driven KRAS amplifications to rapid resistance and showed BBI 825 prevents it.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate low‑coverage whole‑genome sequencing with CrisPRO to flag ecDNA‑positive tumors.

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair RNR inhibition with MEK inhibitors for KRAS‑amplified resistant clones.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted MEK inhibitors remain effective against KRAS‑amplified models.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict MAPK re‑activation signatures to prioritize MEK combo.

unresolved_questions: Will BBI 825 affect normal proliferating tissues leading to hematologic toxicity?
Can ecDNA status be reliably measured in routine clinical biopsies?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Ruijin Hospital, Shanghai Jiao Tong University School of Medicine
talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting the JAK‑STAT pathway sensitizes KRAS mutant cancer to KRAS inhibitors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the compound's effect is related to the activation level of STAT3"
Speaker said: "we don't see any adverse effect" regarding the STAT3 inhibitor

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: JAK1/STAT3 pathway up‑regulation provides bypass signaling
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: we observed high phospho‑STAT3 in resistant lines without clear upstream driver

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No STAT3 activation biomarker used for patient selection

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Early phase trial; limited safety data and potential class‑wide toxicities
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on STAT3 activation which may be heterogeneous

key_data_gaps: Clinical validation of STAT3 inhibitor safety

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to generate a phospho‑STAT3 activation score to select patients for KRAS + STAT3 inhibitor combos.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that STAT3 inhibitor efficacy correlates with phospho‑STAT3 levels.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate phosphoproteomics with CrisPRO predictive modeling for STAT3‑dependent resistance.

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair KRAS G12D inhibitors with JAK1 inhibitors (e.g., tofacitinib) as an alternative to STAT3 inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated tofacitinib blocks phospho‑STAT3 and improves viability.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can rank JAK inhibitors based on predicted synergy with KRAS blockade.

unresolved_questions: What is the safety profile of the oral STAT3 inhibitor in humans?
Does STAT3 inhibition affect immune microenvironment in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: VEGFR2 blockade overcomes acquired KRAS G12D inhibitor resistance driven by PI3Kγ activation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we think this result a general mechanism in resistance"
Speaker said: "the combination showed significantly greater tumor growth inhibition"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: PI3Kγ‑driven VEGF‑A/VEGFR2 autocrine loop sustains survival
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some resistant models showed rapid progression despite KRAS inhibition

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No selection for PI3Kγ activation or VEGF‑A high tumors

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Monoclonal antibody may have limited tumor penetration
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires combination with KRAS inhibitor; single agent ineffective

key_data_gaps: Clinical validation of VEGFR2 blockade in KRAS‑G12D patients
Safety of long‑term VEGFR2 inhibition combined with KRAS inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a CrisPRO‑derived PI3Kγ/VEGF‑A activation signature to select patients for KRAS + VEGFR2 combo.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker linked PI3Kγ activation and VEGF‑A upregulation to resistance.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate phospho‑proteomics with CrisPRO to predict VEGF‑driven resistance.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine KRAS G12D inhibitors with PI3Kγ selective inhibitors as an alternative to VEGFR2 antibodies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Inhibition of PI3Kγ decreased VEGF‑A and restored sensitivity.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can rank PI3Kγ inhibitors for synergy with KRAS blockade.

cited_competitors.name: Revolution Medicine RMC‑9805
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a trial combining VEGF‑R1 bispecific with KRAS inhibitor

unresolved_questions: Is the VEGFR2‑driven mechanism present in KRAS G12C patients?
What is the impact on normal vasculature with chronic VEGFR2 blockade?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeted therapy‑induced chromosomal instability dictates mitotic dependency on Aurora kinase A
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we cannot show this data as of this presentation"
Speaker said: "the combination can cause tumor regression as well as improve the relapse‑free survival"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Induced chromosomal instability creates a mitotic vulnerability that is not exploited by KRAS inhibition alone
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some cell lines showed only cytostatic response

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No assessment of CIN or micronuclei status to enrich for Aurora A dependency

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Highly selective but may have dose‑limiting toxicities; prior pan‑Aurora programs halted
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires precise patient selection based on CIN

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that KRAS inhibition induces DNA damage and CIN, yet concluded that KRAS inhibitors alone have limited efficacy without addressing the mitotic vulnerability

key_data_gaps: Clinical validation of CIN as predictive biomarker
Safety of low‑dose Aurora A inhibition in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop CrisPRO CIN signature to identify tumors where KRAS inhibition will create Aurora A dependency.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker linked micronuclei induction to Aurora A inhibitor synergy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to integrate γ‑H2AX and micronucleus data for patient enrichment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO's ability to predict synthetic lethal interactions between targeted therapy‑induced stress and cell‑cycle kinases.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed mechanistic link between KRAS‑induced DNA damage and Aurora A dependency.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model stress‑induced synthetic vulnerabilities across drug classes.

cited_competitors.name: Eli Lilly KRAS G12C + Aurora A combo
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as not moving forward due to toxicity concerns

unresolved_questions: Will transient CIN trigger immune activation that could be leveraged with immunotherapy?
What is the therapeutic window for Aurora A inhibition in patients with high CIN?
